document.title: CLINICAL EVALUATION: A GUIDE FOR MANUFACTURERS AND NOTIFIED BODIES UNDER DIRECTIVES 93/42/EEC and 90/385/EEC

sourceSummary.oneLine: European Commission MEDDEV guidance on clinical evaluation for medical devices under Directives 93/42/EEC and 90/385/EEC.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports a common approach for manufacturers and notified bodies; the guide states it is not legally binding and only the Directives are authentic in law.
keyPoints: Clinical evaluation is required for all classes of medical devices and is a manufacturer responsibility.
Clinical evaluation is an ongoing procedure to collect, appraise and analyse clinical data and confirm compliance with relevant Essential Requirements.
The clinical evaluation process is structured into scope definition, data identification, data appraisal, data analysis and finalisation of the clinical evaluation report.
Clinical evaluation must be actively updated with post-market surveillance data.
Notified bodies assess clinical evaluation reports and supporting documentation when required by conformity assessment procedures.

entities.name: European Commission
entities.evidence: EUROPEAN COMMISSION DG Internal Market, Industry, Entrepreneurship and SMEs

entities.name: Directive 2007/47/EC
entities.evidence: These guidelines incorporate changes introduced by Directive 2007/47/EC amending Council Directive 90/385/EEC and Council Directive 93/42/EEC.

entities.name: Manufacturers
entities.evidence: A GUIDE FOR MANUFACTURERS AND NOTIFIED BODIES

entities.name: Notified bodies
entities.evidence: The notified body plays a key role in the assessment and verification of clinical evaluation reports and supporting documentation provided by medical device manufacturers

entities.name: EN ISO 14155:2011
entities.evidence: EN ISO 14155:2011 Clinical investigation of medical devices for human subjects – Good clinical practice

entities.name: EN ISO 14971:2012
entities.evidence: EN ISO 14971:2012 Medical devices – application of risk management to medical devices

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation
concepts.evidence: Clinical evaluation is a methodologically sound ongoing procedure to collect, appraise and analyse clinical data pertaining to a medical device

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation report
concepts.evidence: The clinical evaluation report summarises and draws together the evaluation of all the relevant clinical data documented or referenced in other parts of the technical documentation.

concepts.name: Clinical evidence
concepts.evidence: Clinical evidence: the clinical data and the clinical evaluation report pertaining to a medical device.

concepts.name: Demonstration of equivalence
concepts.evidence: Clinical, technical and biological characteristics shall be taken into consideration for the demonstration of equivalence

concepts.name: Essential Requirements
concepts.evidence: the demonstration of conformity with Essential Requirements for a medical device must include a clinical evaluation

concepts.name: Literature search protocol
concepts.evidence: A literature search and other retrieval of data are carried out based on a search protocol.

evidence.claim: MEDDEV 2.7/1 revision 4 was published in June 2016.
The guide applies to medical devices regulated by Directives 90/385/EEC and 93/42/EEC and does not concern in vitro diagnostic devices.
evidence.evidenceText: This document promotes a common approach to clinical evaluation for medical devices regulated by directives 90/385/EEC and 93/42/EEC. It does not concern in vitro diagnostic devices.

evidence.claim: The MEDDEV guide is not legally binding.
evidence.evidenceText: This guide is not legally binding; only the text of the Directives is authentic in law.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation is required for all classes of medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: The requirements for clinical evaluation apply to all classes of medical devices.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation is the responsibility of the manufacturer and the clinical evaluation report is part of the technical documentation.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical evaluation is a responsibility of the manufacturer and the clinical evaluation report is an element of the technical documentation of a medical device.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation is mandatory for initial CE marking and must be actively updated thereafter.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical evaluation is mandatory for initial CE-marking and it must be actively updated thereafter.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation is conducted throughout the medical device life cycle as an ongoing process.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical evaluation is conducted throughout the life cycle of a medical device, as an ongoing process.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation stages are scope definition, data identification, data appraisal, data analysis and finalisation of the clinical evaluation report.
evidence.evidenceText: There are discrete stages in performing a clinical evaluation: Stage 0: Define the scope... Stage 1: Identify pertinent data. Stage 2: Appraise each individual data set... Stage 3: Analyse the data... Stage 4: Finalise the clinical evaluation report

evidence.claim: The manufacturer should define and justify the frequency of active clinical evaluation updates.
evidence.evidenceText: The manufacturer should define and justify the frequency at which the clinical evaluation needs to be actively updated.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation should be conducted by a suitably qualified individual or team.
evidence.evidenceText: The clinical evaluation should be conducted by a suitably qualified individual or a team.

evidence.claim: Data generated and held by the manufacturer need to be identified and made available to evaluators.
evidence.evidenceText: All data generated and held by the manufacturer need to be identified. Complete data need to be entirely disclosed and made available to the evaluators

evidence.claim: Literature searching is used to identify clinical evaluation data not held by the manufacturer.
evidence.evidenceText: Literature searching is used to identify data not held by the manufacturer that are needed for the clinical evaluation.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation report should be sufficiently detailed for an independent party such as a regulatory authority or notified body.
evidence.evidenceText: The clinical evaluation report should contain sufficient information to be read and understood by an independent party (e.g. regulatory authority or notified body).

evidence.claim: Notified bodies assess and verify clinical evaluation reports and supporting documentation.
evidence.evidenceText: The notified body plays a key role in the assessment and verification of clinical evaluation reports and supporting documentation provided by medical device manufacturers

evidence.claim: Equivalence assessment considers clinical, technical and biological characteristics.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical, technical and biological characteristics shall be taken into consideration for the demonstration of equivalence

reviewFlags.reason: No product models are present because the source is horizontal guidance and is not device-specific.
The document states that it is legally not binding and that only the text of the Directives is authentic in law.
